title: Intensely Flavored Slovakian Meat Stew
description: This Slovakian meat stew is a hearty, one-pot dish featuring rose water and deep-fry-tod, with a focus on grassy and aerated flavors, and is FODMAP-friendly.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat.
Add the beef to the pot and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it starts to soften, about 3 minutes.
Add the chopped carrot and potato to the pot and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the rose water, deep-fry-tod, salt, and black pepper to the pot and stir to combine.
Pour in enough water to cover the ingredients and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the stew, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the meat is tender.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
